title: Sales Tax Calculator
shortDescription: Enter a subtotal and sales tax rate to get the tax amount and final total.
metaDescription: Add sales tax to a subtotal and see the tax amount plus final total for purchases, receipts, and estimates.
intro: Enter the amount before tax and the rate to get the tax added and the full total.
overview:
Sales tax can change the amount you actually pay from the price you first see. This calculator applies the tax rate to your pre-tax subtotal and shows both the tax amount and the final total, so you can check a purchase, receipt, or estimate before you commit.

It is useful when a store, cart, or invoice shows a price before tax, or when you need to compare totals across different locations or rate scenarios. The result is a calculation aid only; invoices, filings, and tax treatment still depend on the rules in the relevant jurisdiction.
howItWorks: Enter the pre-tax amount, such as a product price, cart subtotal, or invoice line.
Enter the sales tax rate as a percentage, such as 6, 7.5, or 8.25.
Review the tax amount and the tax-inclusive final price.

useCases.title: Store checkout estimate
useCases.description: Check the final amount on a shelf-priced item before paying at the register.

useCases.title: Cart total comparison
useCases.description: Compare a pre-tax cart subtotal with the amount after a local or marketplace tax rate is applied.

useCases.title: Receipt line check
useCases.description: See whether a tax line on a receipt matches the subtotal and the rate you expected.

examples.title: Retail item at 8.25%
examples.description: A shelf price becomes a higher checkout total once tax is added.

examples.title: Service purchase at 6%
examples.description: Useful for software, media, or subscription checkouts that add tax after location is known.

examples.title: Invoice line at 7.5%
examples.description: Helps prepare a customer estimate before issuing the final bill.

faq.question: Does this tool add tax to the amount I enter?
faq.answer: Yes. Enter the pre-tax subtotal and the rate, and it returns the added tax amount plus the final total.

faq.question: Can I use any sales tax rate?
faq.answer: Yes. You can calculate with any percentage rate your situation requires, including rates that vary by city, state, or marketplace.

faq.question: Should shipping or fees be included in the subtotal?
faq.answer: Only if those charges are part of the taxable base in your case. Whether they are taxed depends on the product, fee type, and jurisdiction.

faq.question: Why might the result differ from a receipt or invoice?
faq.answer: Receipts may round line items differently or apply tax to a broader base. Some systems also calculate tax after discounts, fees, or shipping.

faq.question: Can I use this for filing taxes?
faq.answer: Use it for estimates and checks only. Official filings and invoices must follow the current rules that apply to the sale.
